elasticity.notes: Staple-driven category with moderate elasticity. Consumers stock up on pantry items during promotions. Cross-category impact is low since grocery items serve distinct meal-prep functions. Q4 sees a mild lift from holiday cooking. Discounts beyond 20% provide diminishing returns as demand ceiling is relatively fixed.

Premium category with low price sensitivity — buyers value quality and brand. Within-category cannibalization is low since products serve different occasions (cheese boards, condiments, snacking). Q4 holiday gifting and entertaining drives a 25% lift. Deep discounts (>20%) can erode brand perception without proportional volume gains.

Highly elastic impulse category. Consumers readily switch between beverages when one is promoted. Summer months (Q2-Q3) see 15-20% above-average volume. Coffee and tea compete directly as hot drink alternatives. Juice, kombucha, and smoothie compete as cold health drinks. Post-promo dips can be significant due to pantry loading.

Moderate-high elasticity driven by impulse purchasing. Salty snacks (chips, popcorn, pretzels) are highly substitutable. Healthy snacks (nuts, granola bars) form a separate sub-cluster with their own cross-elasticity. Q1 dip from post-holiday health resolutions. Super Bowl (early Q1) and summer BBQ season (Q2-Q3) drive spikes. Party occasions in Q4 maintain steady demand.
